description.en: Delaware C-Corp Certificate of Incorporation — the document filed with the Secretary of State to bring the company into existence under the Delaware General Corporation Law.

plainDescription.en: The legal name under which the company will be incorporated in Delaware. Must end in a corporate indicator such as 'Inc.', 'Corporation', or 'Company'.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(1) requires the certificate to set forth the name of the corporation. The name must be distinguishable from other entities registered in Delaware and must contain an approved corporate designator.

label.en: Use the company name as entered

plainDescription.en: Uses the company name exactly as you entered it when starting this journey.

en: Matches your brand exactly
Matches your brand exactly
Confirm the Secretary of State's name availability before filing
Confirm the Secretary of State's name availability before filing

legalText.en: FIRST: The name of this corporation is [company-name].

plainDescription.en: Every Delaware corporation must have a registered agent in Delaware to accept service of process. Most startups use a commercial registered agent service.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(2) requires the certificate to set forth the address of the corporation's registered office in Delaware and the name of its registered agent at that address.

label.en: Use the registered agent as entered

plainDescription.en: Uses the registered agent and address you provided. Common choices: Harvard Business Services, Corporation Service Company, CT Corporation.

en: Standard commercial registered agent covers statutory requirements
Standard commercial registered agent covers statutory requirements
Annual registered-agent fee (~$50–$300)
Annual registered-agent fee (~$50–$300)

legalText.en: SECOND: The address of the corporation's registered office in the State of Delaware is [registered-agent]. The corporation's registered agent at that address is so designated in accordance with 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(2).

plainDescription.en: Most modern Delaware C-Corps use a broad, all-purpose statement so the corporation can engage in any lawful business without amending the certificate later.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(3) permits a broad purpose statement. The all-purpose language below is standard for Delaware startups and preferred by YC, NVCA, and most institutional investors.

plainDescription.en: Engages in any lawful act or activity. Gives maximum flexibility without needing to amend the certificate as the business evolves.

en: Maximum business flexibility
Standard for venture-backed startups
No need to amend when pivoting
Maximum business flexibility
Standard for venture-backed startups
No need to amend when pivoting
None — this is the default for modern Delaware C-Corps
None — this is the default for modern Delaware C-Corps

legalText.en: THIRD: The purpose of the corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law of the State of Delaware.

plainDescription.en: How many shares of Common Stock can the corporation issue? This sets the ceiling — you can issue up to this number without amending the certificate, but amending later requires a board + shareholder vote.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(4) requires the certificate to state the total authorized shares. Amending later means filing a Certificate of Amendment with the Delaware Secretary of State, plus board and stockholder approval. Franchise tax: Delaware uses the lower of two calculations — at the typical low par value ($0.00001) the 'assumed par value method' gives roughly the $85 minimum regardless of authorized count, so share count mainly affects headroom, not tax.

plainDescription.en: The YC / NVCA standard. Leaves room for founders (typically 5-7M), an option pool (typically 1-2M), a seed round, and a Series A without amending the certificate.

en: Industry-standard ceiling that investors expect
Fits founders + 10-20% option pool + multiple priced rounds
No meaningful Delaware franchise tax impact at low par value
Industry-standard ceiling that investors expect
Fits founders + 10-20% option pool + multiple priced rounds
No meaningful Delaware franchise tax impact at low par value
Marginally larger-sounding cap table on day one (investors care about issued, not authorized)
Marginally larger-sounding cap table on day one (investors care about issued, not authorized)

legalText.en: FOURTH: The total number of shares of stock the corporation shall have authority to issue is 10,000,000 shares of Common Stock, each having a par value of $[par-value] per share.

plainDescription.en: Tighter ceiling. Fine for a bootstrapped or lifestyle business, or a founder team that doesn't expect institutional rounds. If you later raise a priced round, you'll almost certainly need to amend the certificate.

en: Simpler-looking cap table
Less nominal dilution headroom on paper
Simpler-looking cap table
Less nominal dilution headroom on paper
Amending the certificate later is extra paperwork + filing fees
Investors prefer seeing 10M as the default
Cramped if you need a reasonable option pool plus multiple rounds
Amending the certificate later is extra paperwork + filing fees
Investors prefer seeing 10M as the default
Cramped if you need a reasonable option pool plus multiple rounds

legalText.en: FOURTH: The total number of shares of stock the corporation shall have authority to issue is 5,000,000 shares of Common Stock, each having a par value of $[par-value] per share.

plainDescription.en: Extra headroom. Sensible if you expect several priced rounds over the first few years or want to avoid amending the certificate. No real downside at typical low par values.

en: Room for many future rounds without certificate amendment
Simpler administration if you grow fast
Still well below where franchise-tax calculation methods diverge meaningfully
Room for many future rounds without certificate amendment
Simpler administration if you grow fast
Still well below where franchise-tax calculation methods diverge meaningfully
Non-standard — some investors will ask why you deviated from 10M
Non-standard — some investors will ask why you deviated from 10M

legalText.en: FOURTH: The total number of shares of stock the corporation shall have authority to issue is 20,000,000 shares of Common Stock, each having a par value of $[par-value] per share.

plainDescription.en: Delaware law allows the certificate to limit directors' personal liability for monetary damages arising from breaches of fiduciary duty, except for breaches of the duty of loyalty, intentional misconduct, or improper distributions.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(b)(7) authorizes this exculpation clause. Nearly all Delaware C-Corps include it to help attract qualified directors.

label.en: Limit director liability to the fullest extent permitted by DGCL

plainDescription.en: Exculpates directors for monetary damages except in the narrow cases DGCL § 102(b)(7) disallows (duty of loyalty, intentional misconduct, etc.).

en: Standard for Delaware C-Corps
Helps recruit independent directors
Standard for Delaware C-Corps
Helps recruit independent directors
None — this is the market standard
None — this is the market standard

legalText.en: FIFTH: To the fullest extent permitted by the General Corporation Law of the State of Delaware as it now exists or as it may be amended, no director of this corporation shall be personally liable to the corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages arising from a breach of fiduciary duty as a director. Any repeal or modification of this provision shall be prospective only and shall not adversely affect any right or protection of a director existing at the time of such repeal or modification.

plainDescription.en: The person signing the certificate into existence. Usually a single founder or the company's outside counsel. The incorporator's job ends once the initial board is elected.

legalContext.en: 8 Del. C. § 102(a)(6) requires the name and mailing address of the incorporator. Under § 108 the incorporator has the power to adopt initial bylaws and elect the initial directors if those are not named in the certificate.

plainDescription.en: Uses the founder flagged as incorporator (or the primary founder by default).

en: Single-signer convenience; common pattern for early-stage formation
Single-signer convenience; common pattern for early-stage formation
Incorporator's role ends at initial board election — they don't retain special authority afterward
Incorporator's role ends at initial board election — they don't retain special authority afterward

legalText.en: SIXTH: The name and mailing address of the incorporator is [incorporator], c/o the registered office set forth in Article SECOND.
